competitor_patterns: Top-ranking pages (Cyclist.co.uk, BikeSafetyGear) exceed 6,000+ words with explicit 'how we test' methodology sections that build E-E-A-T trust signals — our longest posts cap at ~6,400 words but lack a named tester/reviewer authority voice

Rudy Project and Cyclist both use H2s structured around buyer decision stages ('How do I choose the right cycling sunglasses?') rather than brand-centric headings, directly matching informational search intent at the top of the funnel

All top-3 competitors combine reviews + FAQ schema + comparison tables in a single page — BikeSafetyGear's interchangeable lens post ranks #1 with a table present; our interchangeable-lens pages at 1,088–1,712 words lack sufficient depth to compete

Rudy Project leverages brand authority signals ('Made in Italy', '24/48h delivery') in the meta description itself — converting brand equity into snippet CTR; our snippets are product-feature-led rather than urgency/trust-led

Cyclist.co.uk's H2 structure includes a dedicated 'tested and reviewed' credibility section near the top, signalling first-hand experience to both Google's Helpful Content system and readers scanning for trustworthy sources
our_gaps: 19 of our 41 pages are under 1,300 words — thin content that cannot compete with 4,000–6,600 word competitor roundups for high-impression queries like 'best cycling glasses 2026' (266 impressions, pos 7.8); these short pages dilute crawl budget and fail to satisfy comprehensive search intent

FAQ schema coverage is only 33% (roughly 14/41 pages) — the majority of our short-form pages (gravel-vs-road, photochromic, anti-fog variants, fit guides) have no FAQ schema, leaving rich-result opportunities untapped for queries where Google shows FAQ snippets

Zero comparison tables on our highest-traffic Oakley-alternatives pages that lack them (best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked at 3,924 words has no table) — competitors use tables as the primary decision-aid format and Google rewards structured comparison data

Our brand-misspelling query 'veluto' (248 impressions, 0.8% CTR, pos 5.6) has no dedicated landing page or redirect strategy — 246 impressions are being wasted with only 2 clicks, suggesting our title tags for brand queries do not correct/acknowledge the misspelling to capture intent

No content covers 'how to choose cycling glasses' as a standalone comprehensive guide matching Cyclist.co.uk's H2 structure — our buying guide exists but is framed around '2026' and product picks rather than the evergreen decision-framework that earns editorial links and AI citations
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses review 2026 — BikeSafetyGear ranks #1 for this; our existing interchangeable-lens pages are 1,088–1,712 words and lack the depth to compete; direct StradaPro VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione system is a perfect match

road cycling glasses reviewed 2026 — Cyclist.co.uk dominates with 6,632 words; a long-form, tester-voice, criteria-led review post with named ride conditions would target this gap and support our pos-7.8 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' query

cycling glasses for sweaty rides anti-fog — implied by Cyclist's Unobtanium/sweat snippet; our anti-fog pages exist but are too short and don't use this exact conversational phrasing that matches summer long-tail intent

lightweight cycling glasses under 30g — no competitor owns this specific weight-threshold query; Velluto's 25g frame is the single strongest differentiator and this long-tail has clear purchase intent with minimal competition

cycling glasses 30-day trial risk-free — almost no competitor offers this; it targets bottom-of-funnel buyers with purchase anxiety and could rank for zero-click AI answers about 'best cycling glasses return policy 2026'
next_post_guidelines: Target the 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' cluster (266 impressions, pos 7.8) with a 5,500+ word tested-and-ranked roundup structured around Cyclist.co.uk's H2 pattern: open with 'how we test', follow with ranked picks table, close with a buyer decision framework — include FAQ schema on at least 5 questions

Address the 'veluto' misspelling (248 impressions, 0.8% CTR) by creating a brand landing page or ensuring the homepage meta title includes 'Velluto (also searched: Veluto)' style phrasing, and internally link from all blog posts to capture navigational intent leaking to low-CTR position 5.6

For every post targeting 'oakley alternative(s)' queries (combined 331 impressions), lead the meta description with a concrete differentiator stat: '25g frame, 30-day trial, €X' — not a generic brand statement; CTR is 1.6–3.0% at positions 6–8, meaning the snippet is not converting the impression

Every new post must include a comparison table even if comparing only 3 products — GSC shows our table-present pages perform better and all top-3 competitors use tables; prioritise adding tables to the 5 highest-impression pages that currently lack them

Write at least one post per week in a genuine first-person tester voice ('After 400km in the Dolomites, here is what failed and what held up') — this satisfies Google's E-E-A-T 'Experience' signal that competitors like Cyclist.co.uk exploit and that AI Overview citations reward

Expand all sub-1,300 word pages that target near-top keywords to a minimum of 2,500 words before creating new pages — specifically: gravel-vs-road (1,037w), photochromic (1,104w), anti-fog-summer (1,037w), cycling-glasses-under-150 (1,088w); thin pages at positions 6–9 are one content expansion away from top-3

Build one dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro vs [Competitor]' post per week using the exact competitor brand name in the H1 and URL slug — GSC shows 'oakley alternatives' and 'best oakley alternatives' together generate 332 impressions; branded comparison URLs capture high-intent navigational + comparative queries simultaneously
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to every page currently missing it — 27+ pages have no FAQ schema; this is a 10-minute implementation per page using existing FAQ content and directly increases rich-result eligibility for queries where Google displays FAQ snippets (anti-fog, interchangeable lens, fit guides all qualify)

Add a 3-column comparison table (Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley vs budget option) to every page over 2,500 words that currently lacks one — specifically best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked (3,924w, no table) and are-expensive-cycling-glasses-worth-it-honest-answer (3,806w, no table); tables improve dwell time, enable featured snippet eligibility, and match competitor structure

Standardise meta description format across all pages to the template: '[Specific stat or differentiator] — [content promise] — [trust signal or CTA]' e.g. 'At 25g, StradaPro beats Oakley on weight and price. Tested over 400km. 30-day risk-free trial.' — current snippets are generic and the low CTRs (0.8–3.0%) on high-impression queries confirm they are not compelling searchers to click
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.2, CTR 3.0%) → New title: Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: Tested & Ranked | Why: Adds 'Tested & Ranked' credibility signal and year to match searcher intent for a definitive list; keeps under 60 chars and increases CTR vs generic brand titles

QUERY: 'veluto' (pos 5.6, CTR 0.8%) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Store | StradaPro | Why: Corrects the misspelling implicitly by showing the correct brand name clearly; 'Official Store' captures navigational intent and explains the low CTR caused by uncertainty about which result is the real brand

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses' (pos 8.3, CTR 2.6%) → New title: Best Cycling Glasses 2026: 25g, Anti-Fog, Tested | Why: Injects a concrete spec (25g) and feature (Anti-Fog) that competitors' generic titles omit, differentiating the snippet and targeting cyclists scanning for specific performance claims

QUERY: 'oakley alternative' (pos 7.6, CTR 1.6%) → New title: The Best Oakley Alternative 2026 – Save €80+ | Why: Price-saving angle ('Save €80+') directly addresses the core motivation behind searching for an alternative and creates urgency; singular 'alternative' matches exact query form
content_quick_wins: QUERY 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 7.8) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested Over 500km — Full Ranked List' | Add: Expand to 5,500+ words with a 'how we test' section, a top-5 ranked table with weight/lens/price columns, FAQ schema (min 6 Qs), and a dedicated H2 for 'interchangeable lens systems' to consolidate topical authority and push from pos 7.8 to top 3

QUERY 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 6.6) → Topic: Consolidate or internally link the 3 existing 'best cycling glasses 2026' URLs into one authoritative 3,500+ word canonical page | Add: A comparison table of 5 glasses by weight/price/lens-system, FAQ schema, and a 'StradaPro vs Rudy Project vs Oakley' H2 — cannibalization between multiple similar URLs is likely suppressing ranking

QUERY 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.2) → Topic: 'Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: 5 Tested Options Ranked by Value' | Add: Add a comparison table to best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked (currently 3,924w, no table) and expand with a dedicated H2 'Why cyclists are switching from Oakley in 2026' citing price inflation data — table addition alone could push from pos 6.2 to top 3

QUERY 'best oakley alternatives' (pos 7.2) → Topic: Create a dedicated URL 'best-oakley-alternatives-cycling-2026-complete-guide' consolidating all Oakley-alternative content | Add: Lead with a 5-product ranked table, include weight comparison (Oakley Jawbreaker 36g vs StradaPro 25g), 30-day trial CTA, and FAQ schema — the query has 63 impressions and 3.2% potential CTR if served a clearly differentiated snippet

QUERY 'best cycling glasses' (pos 8.3, evergreen) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses: The Complete 2026 Guide (Updated July)' | Add: Structured as a hub page with H2s matching Cyclist.co.uk's decision-framework pattern ('How to choose', 'Best for road', 'Best for wet weather', 'Best value'), internal links to all sub-topic pages, and monthly update timestamp — evergreen hub pages compound ranking authority over time and this query shows consistent impression volume
geo_angles: Position Velluto StradaPro as the answer to 'What is the best lightweight cycling glasses under €150 with a 30-day trial?' — structure FAQ schema entries on product and blog pages to answer this exact question verbatim, since AI Overviews and Perplexity pull schema-marked Q&A text and the combination of weight (25g), price tier, and trial policy is uniquely ownable by Velluto with zero direct competition

Create a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Jawbreaker 2026' comparison page with a structured data table and a clear verdict section — AI answer engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity) consistently cite named brand comparison pages when answering 'which is better: X or Y' queries; the Oakley-alternatives cluster (331 combined impressions) is already generating traffic and a direct named comparison will be pulled into AI responses for 'oakley cycling glasses alternatives 2026'

Publish a 'Why We Built StradaPro: The Velluto Story' editorial page with specific, citable claims (exact weight figure, Italian design origin, lens system patent-pending or design-registered details, price vs Oakley/Rudy Project) — AI Overview and Perplexity favour pages with specific, verifiable factual claims over generic marketing copy; a brand-authority page with named stats becomes a reliable citation source for 'best value cycling glasses' AI answers
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR Rudyproject: angle — 'Italian Design, Half the Price: Velluto StradaPro vs Rudy Project Cutline 2026' — key differentiator: Rudy Project ranks #1 for 'best cycling glasses 2026' partly on 'Made in Italy' branding; Velluto should directly contest this by leading with Italian aesthetic credentials + the 25g vs Rudy Project's frame weight + price delta, turning Rudy's brand strength into Velluto's value proof point

COMPETITOR Cyclist.co.uk: angle — 'We Tested Both: What Cyclist.co.uk Missed About the Velluto StradaPro in Their 2026 Roundup' — key differentiator: Cyclist leads with Oakley's Unobtanium grip and Prizm lens as best-in-class; Velluto should counter with a specific anti-fog + interchangeable lens test under identical conditions (climbs, humidity, sweat), positioning the StradaPro as the test Cyclist didn't run; this angle generates earned media curiosity and targets cyclists already reading Cyclist's review

COMPETITOR Bikesafetygear.com: angle — 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Velluto StradaPro vs the Top 5 — Click-In System Tested' — key differentiator: BikeSafetyGear ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a 5-product roundup; Velluto should publish a direct competing post leading with the VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione tool-free click-in system as the fastest lens swap tested, with a step-by-step video description and time-to-swap comparison table — the 'interchangeable lens' keyword cluster is a direct product feature match and highest-converting long-tail for StradaPro
